text: Jesus Heals the Man Born Blind

segments.text: Now as Jesus was passing by, He saw a man blind from birth,
and His disciples asked Him,
Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus answered,
Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but this happened so that the works of God would be displayed in him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While it is daytime, we must do the works of Him who sent Me. Night is coming, when no one can work.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When Jesus had said this, He spit on the ground, made some mud, and applied it to the man’s eyes.
Then He told him,
Go, wash in the Pool of Siloam
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: (which means
Sent
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ). So the man went and washed, and came back seeing.
At this, his neighbors and those who had formerly seen him begging began to ask,
Isn’t this the man who used to sit and beg?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Some claimed that he was, but others said,
No, he just looks like him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the man kept saying,
I am the one.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How then were your eyes opened?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
He answered,
The man they call Jesus made some mud and anointed my eyes, and He told me to go to Siloam and wash. So I went and washed and received my sight.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Where is He?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
I do not know,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he answered.
They brought to the Pharisees the man who had been blind.
Now the day on which Jesus had made the mud and opened his eyes was a Sabbath.

So the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight.
The man answered,
He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and now I can see.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees also

segments.text: Because of this, some of the Pharisees said,
This man is not from God, for He does not keep the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: others

segments.text: But others said,
How can a sinful man perform such signs?
segments.speaker: others

segments.text: And there was division among them.
So once again they asked the man who had been blind,
What do you say about Him, since it was your eyes He opened?
segments.speaker: others

segments.text: He is a prophet,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: the man replied.
The Jews still did not believe that the man had been blind and had received his sight until they summoned his parents
and asked,
Is this your son, the one you say was born blind? So how is it that he can now see?
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: His parents answered,
We know he is our son, and we know he was born blind.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: But how he can now see or who opened his eyes, we do not know. Ask him. He is old enough to speak for himself.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews. For the Jews had already determined that anyone who confessed Jesus as the Christ would be put out of the synagogue.
That was why his parents said,
He is old enough. Ask him.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: So a second time they called for the man who had been blind and said,
Give glory to God! We know that this man is a sinner.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: He answered,
Whether He is a sinner I do not know. There is one thing I do know: I was blind, but now I see!
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: What did He do to you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
How did He open your eyes?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He replied,
I already told you, and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become His disciples?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then they heaped insults on him and said,
You are His disciple; we are disciples of Moses.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we do not know where this man is from.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: That is remarkable indeed!
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: the man said.
You do not know where He is from, and yet He opened my eyes.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but He does listen to the one who worships Him and does His will.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Never before has anyone heard of opening the eyes of a man born blind.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: If this man were not from God, He could do no such thing.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: They replied,
You were born in utter sin, and you are instructing us?
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: And they threw him out.
When Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, He found the man and said,
Do you believe in the Son of Man?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Who is He, Sir?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he replied.
Tell me so that I may believe in Him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You have already seen Him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus answered.
He is the One speaking with you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, I believe,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said. And he worshiped Jesus.
Then Jesus declared,
For judgment I have come into this world, so that the blind may see and those who see may become blind.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Some of the Pharisees who were with Him heard this, and they asked Him,
Are we blind too?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you were blind,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
you would not be guilty of sin. But since you claim you can see, your guilt remains.
segments.speaker: Jesus
